turns.text: Welcome to Acme Corp's Q3 2024 earnings call. Joining us today are CEO Jane Mitchell and CFO Robert Lee.

Thank you. We delivered another strong quarter. Revenue grew 14% year over year to $2.8 billion, operating margin expanded 180 basis points to 28.4%, and free cash flow was $720 million. Our Cloud segment continues to lead the growth story, with revenue up 32% to $980 million.

For the fourth quarter, we expect revenue of $2.95 to $3.05 billion, representing 11 to 14% growth. We continue to expect full year operating margin to expand 150 to 200 basis points. Our previously announced $1 billion cost optimization program is on track to deliver the full target by year end.
We will now begin the question-and-answer session. Our first question comes from Sarah Chen at Goldman Sachs.
Thanks for taking my question. Could you give us the Cloud segment operating margin in the quarter and how it trended sequentially?

Cloud operating margin was 21.4% in Q3, up from 19.8% in Q2 and 16.2% in the year-ago period. The 160 basis points of sequential improvement was driven by scale leverage and continued infrastructure efficiency.
What's the magnitude of the AI-related revenue contribution in Q3, and how should we think about that scaling into 2025?

We're incredibly excited about the AI opportunity in front of us. Customer demand has been extraordinary, and the team is executing brilliantly. We have multi-year roadmaps with our top 100 customers that all include AI components, and the platform momentum has never been stronger.
Can you quantify the percentage of Cloud revenue coming from your top 10 customers, and how that concentration has changed?

We don't disclose customer concentration at that level of granularity. What I can say is that our customer base is well diversified across industries and geographies. We'll get back to you with any additional color we can share offline.
On the cost optimization program, how much of the $1 billion has hit the P&L through Q3, and what's the cadence for Q4?

We've delivered approximately $700 million of the $1 billion through the first three quarters. The remainder will flow through Q4. We're not breaking down the specific bucket-level cadence at this time, but we feel very confident in the full year target.
Are you considering any changes to the dividend policy given the strong free cash flow?

Capital allocation is something the board reviews regularly. We're focused on driving long-term shareholder value, and we've consistently invested in the highest-return opportunities across organic growth, M&A, and shareholder returns. Our framework hasn't changed.
One follow-up — what was the FX impact on revenue in the quarter?
FX was a 130 basis point headwind to reported revenue growth in Q3. On a constant currency basis, revenue grew 15.3% versus the 14% reported figure.
